generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance MotorActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Physical actuator controlling vehicle propulsion via ROS2 command dissemination with 100ms latency constraint for ACC functionality"
]

instance SteeringServoActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Physical actuator managing vehicle steering via remote control commands through Data Collection and Device Control Entity"
]

instance ActuationFunctionalEntity : DTDFVocab:FunctionalEntity [
    base:desc "ROS2-based functional entity executing actuation commands to MotorActuator and SteeringServoActuator for dynamic vehicle state adjustments"
    base:contains MotorActuator, SteeringServoActuator
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Onboard Hokuyo UST-10LX LiDAR sensor providing 2D/3D environmental mapping and object tracking for ACC system"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudData
]

instance IntelRealsenseD435iCamera : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Onboard Intel RealSense D435i depth camera providing vehicle state estimation and spatial awareness through depth sensor data"
    DTDFVocab:producedData DepthCameraData
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Embedded vehicle state sensors measuring speed, acceleration, and inter-vehicle distance for ACC feedback"
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateMetrics
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Raw point cloud data from HokuyoUST10LX LiDAR transmitted via Wi-Fi UDP through ROS2 network for real-time processing"
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR
]

instance DepthCameraData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Depth sensor data from IntelRealsenseD435i transmitted via Wi-Fi UDP through ROS2 network for spatial awareness calculations"
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IntelRealsenseD435iCamera
]

instance VehicleStateMetrics : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Real-time vehicle state measurements (speed, acceleration, inter-vehicle distance) transmitted via ROS2 network for ACC processing"
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
]

instance PreprocessedLiDARData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Clustered and tracked LiDAR point clouds converted to actionable object tracks and driving path projections via Data Pre-Processing Functional Entity"
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance VehicleDynamicsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "ROS2-based computational reflection of vehicle dynamics using MATLAB/Simulink for trajectory planning and ACC control logic"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MATLABSimulinkEnabler
]

instance ObjectTrackingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Python-based model for real-time object detection and tracking from preprocessed LiDAR data using clustering algorithms"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PythonProcessingEnabler
]

instance LiDARPointCloudDatabase : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured storage of historical and real-time LiDAR point cloud data in SQL database for analysis and replay"
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SQLDatabaseEnabler
]

instance VehicleStateTelemetry : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Continuous stream of vehicle state metrics (speed, acceleration, inter-vehicle distance) for real-time monitoring and ACC processing"
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateMetrics
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Middleware
]

instance ProcessedObjectTracks : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Actionable object tracks and driving path projections derived from preprocessed LiDAR data for ACC decision making"
    DTDFVocab:fromData PreprocessedLiDARData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ObjectTrackingModel
]

// C11: Enablers
instance ROS2Middleware : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Core middleware enabling inter-process communication between all functional entities via ROS2 topics and services with 100ms latency"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeStateMonitoringService, EdgeAwareACCProcessingService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance DataPreProcessingFunctionalEntity : DTDFVocab:FunctionalEntity [
    base:desc "ROS2-based functional entity performing LiDAR point cloud clustering and tracking to convert raw data into actionable object tracks"
    base:contains ObjectTrackingModel
    DTDFVocab:inputTo LiDARPointCloudData
    DTDFVocab:outputTo PreprocessedLiDARData
]

instance DataTranslationFunctionalEntity : DTDFVocab:FunctionalEntity [
    base:desc "ROS2-based functional entity handling data serialization and unit conversion for compatibility across system components"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DepthCameraData, VehicleStateMetrics
    DTDFVocab:outputTo VehicleStateTelemetry
]

instance PythonProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python 3.8 runtime environment executing object tracking algorithms and data processing pipelines for ACC system"
    DTDFVocab:enables ObjectTrackingService
]

instance MATLABSimulinkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MATLAB/Simulink environment for vehicle dynamics modeling and real-time control algorithm execution"
    DTDFVocab:enables ACCControlService
]

instance EdgeComputingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano platform executing edge computations for ACC processing with 100ms latency constraints"
    DTDFVocab:enables EdgeAwareACCProcessingService
]

instance SQLDatabaseEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Structured database storage for historical and real-time sensor data with SQL interface for query operations"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance VisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "3D visualization component using ROS2-based dashboards for real-time monitoring of vehicle state and object tracks"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeVisualizationService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeStateMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous monitoring service providing real-time telemetry of vehicle state variables for ACC decision making with 100ms latency"
    DTDFVocab:provides VehicleStateInsight
]

instance EdgeAwareACCProcessingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Distributed computing service offloading high-complexity ACC computations from edge device to cloud/servers while maintaining real-time constraints"
    DTDFVocab:provides ACCComputationInsight
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Cloud/edge-based service enabling real-time reconfiguration of ACC parameters with 500ms RTT latency and TLS encryption"
    DTDFVocab:provides RemoteControlAction
]

instance ObjectTrackingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time object detection and tracking service converting LiDAR point clouds into actionable driving path projections"
    DTDFVocab:provides ObjectTrackingInsight
]

instance RealTimeVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "3D visualization service providing immediate validation of sensor-derived inputs through ROS2-based dashboards"
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualizationInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance VehicleStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time insight into vehicle speed, acceleration, and inter-vehicle distance for ACC system monitoring"
]

instance ACCComputationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Computational insight into sensor fusion and trajectory optimization results for ACC decision making"
]

instance ObjectTrackingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Actionable insight into detected objects and driving path projections derived from processed LiDAR data"
]

instance RemoteControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic action enabling remote reconfiguration of ACC parameters including minimum following distance and deceleration thresholds"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance EmergencyBrakingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic emergency braking action triggered by cloud/edge system to validate system resilience in edge cases"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C1: Entities
instance PhysicalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:PhysicalTwin [
    base:desc "Decentralized entity hosting physical actuation components, sensors, and data collection functionality on distinct hardware"
    base:contains HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR, IntelRealsenseD435iCamera, VehicleStateSensor, ActuationFunctionalEntity, DataPreProcessingFunctionalEntity, DataTranslationFunctionalEntity
]

instance DigitalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:DigitalTwin [
    base:desc "Entity managing data management, control mechanisms, and computational reflection of physical twin via ROS2-based models"
    base:contains VehicleDynamicsModel, ObjectTrackingModel, ROS2Middleware, EdgeComputingEnabler, SQLDatabaseEnabler
]

instance UserEntity : DTDFVocab:UserInterface [
    base:desc "Entity subdivided into 3D Visualization and Control Panel components for human interaction with the digital twin"
    base:contains VisualizationEnabler, RemoteManagementService
]

// C12: Entity Relationships
instance PhysicalToDigitalDataFlow : DTDFVocab:DataFlow [
    base:desc "Wi-Fi UDP-based data transmission from PhysicalTwinEntity to DigitalTwinEntity via ROS2 network"
    DTDFVocab:fromSource PhysicalTwinEntity
    DTDFVocab:toTarget DigitalTwinEntity
    DTDFVocab:transfers LiDARPointCloudData, DepthCameraData, VehicleStateMetrics
]

instance DigitalToUserInterface : DTDFVocab:DataFlow [
    base:desc "ROS2-based data flow from DigitalTwinEntity to UserEntity for visualization and control panel interactions"
    DTDFVocab:fromSource DigitalTwinEntity
    DTDFVocab:toTarget UserEntity
    DTDFVocab:transfers VehicleStateTelemetry, ProcessedObjectTracks
]

instance ControlCommandFlow : DTDFVocab:DataFlow [
    base:desc "ROS2 service-based command flow from UserEntity to DigitalTwinEntity for remote ACC parameter adjustments"
    DTDFVocab:fromSource UserEntity
    DTDFVocab:toTarget DigitalTwinEntity
    DTDFVocab:transfers RemoteControlAction
]

// C13: Environment
instance AutonomousVehicleEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Operational environment for 1/10-scale autonomous vehicle with real-time cyber-physical system requirements"
    base:contains PhysicalTwinEntity, DigitalTwinEntity, UserEntity
]

instance NetworkInfrastructure : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Wi-Fi-based network infrastructure supporting ROS2 inter-node communication with 100ms latency constraints"
    base:contains PhysicalToDigitalDataFlow, DigitalToUserInterface, ControlCommandFlow
]

// Additional required instances for missing references
instance ACCControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Control service for ACC system executing vehicle dynamics models and trajectory planning"
]

instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service for monitoring and analyzing sensor data and system performance"
]

instance VisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visualization insight providing graphical representation of vehicle state and object tracks"
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in this DTS is governed by the *Controlling FE* (ACC - Adaptive Cruise Control) and *Actuation FE*, which execute commands derived from digital twin logic. **Command types** include **emergency braking** and **modifying the desired speed** (R5), transmitted via the *Data Translation* component as serialized messages. **Control message formats** are standardized through ROS2 QoS policies (implied by system design) and inter-entity communication over Wi-Fi. **Validation and authentication mechanisms** are not explicitly documented, though the *Data Translation* component ensures message alignment and unit consistency. **Feedback systems** are not described, but the system relies on **closed-loop control** where the *Actuation FE* converts desired speed commands into **motor voltage commands** (Table I). **Conflict resolution** is not addressed in the documents, and **command execution confirmation** is not specified beyond the actuation process itself."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin System (DTS) enforces a **100 ms clock synchronization accuracy** between physical and digital components due to hardware limitations, ensuring precise temporal alignment for computation offloading and coordination. Network latency is constrained to **at most 100 ms** from the vehicle to the Digital Twin Entity (DTE) to maintain real-time responsiveness. The system adheres to this temporal granularity to support safety-critical vehicular applications, where stricter standards (e.g., <100 ms) are mandated elsewhere but not explicitly implemented here. No explicit mention of service-specific time scales or sub-millisecond requirements is provided in the documents."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adopts an **ISO 23247 Reference Architecture (RA)-inspired design**, where Functional Entities (FEs) are mapped to ROS2 packages, each containing one or more ROS2 nodes. The architecture emphasizes **entity-based decomposition** with explicit hardware partitioning: the *Physical Twin Entity* (sensors/actuators/compute), *Digital Twin Entity* (data management/control), and *User Entity* (visualization/control). Resource management is implicitly tied to hardware deployment, as each entity operates on distinct hardware, enabling parallel execution. No explicit mention of topic-based or service-based communication patterns, QoS policies, or system-wide orchestration frameworks (e.g., Kubernetes, edge orchestration) is provided. The system’s coordination relies on ROS2’s native inter-node communication within the defined entities."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system emphasizes **real-time synchronization** with a **network latency requirement of at most 100 ms** and **clock synchronization accuracy of 100 ms** to ensure temporal consistency between physical and digital representations. The architecture does not explicitly detail **model fidelity metrics**, **validation methods**, or **uncertainty quantification**, nor does it reference standards like **IEC 61508** or **IEC 62264**. Instead, fidelity is implicitly ensured through **ROS2/Wi-Fi-enabled low-latency communication** and **entity-based decomposition** per ISO 23247. No mention of **Monte Carlo simulations**, **Bayesian methods**, or **code reviews** is present in the provided documents."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **technical network infrastructure** of this DTS is designed for **low-latency, distributed communication** between Physical Twin Entity, Digital Twin Entity, and User Entity. **Communication protocols** include **ROS2** for intra-system SW component interaction (e.g., between Data Pre-Processing FE, Controlling FE, and Actuation FE) and **Wi-Fi with UDP transport** (partially referenced) for inter-entity communication. **Network topology** follows a **distributed architecture**, with computational tasks offloaded to specialized hardware (e.g., NVIDIA Jetson for point cloud processing). **Latency requirements** are strictly constrained to **at most 100 ms** due to hardware limitations, ensuring real-time responsiveness for ACC functionalities. **Bandwidth and reliability mechanisms** are not explicitly quantified, but the system’s reliance on **real-time visualization** and **object tracking** implies sufficient throughput for high-resolution LiDAR data streams. **Security protocols** are not mentioned, though the use of **Wi-Fi** suggests potential vulnerabilities that are not addressed in the provided documents. **Redundancy mechanisms** are not documented, but the system’s critical nature (e.g., autonomous driving) may implicitly require failover strategies for network disruptions.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **deployment infrastructure** of this DTS leverages a **distributed, edge-centric architecture** with specialized hardware for computational offloading. **Hosting platforms** include **on-premise NVIDIA Jetson devices** for the Physical Twin Entity (handling LiDAR processing and actuation) and **external data storage** for object tracks and vehicle state data. **Computational requirements** are not explicitly detailed, but the system’s reliance on **point cloud clustering and tracking** suggests significant processing demands (e.g., handling LiDAR scans with high point densities). **Scalability mechanisms** are implied through the modular design of SW components (e.g., Data Translation FE, Controlling FE), which can be independently scaled based on workload. **Deployment models** are not specified beyond the distributed setup, though the mention of **ROS2** suggests compatibility with containerized or microservices-based deployments. **Availability/reliability assurance** is not addressed, but the **100 ms latency requirement** indicates a need for high-availability hardware and network infrastructure. **Edge-only deployment** is inferred from the use of Jetson devices, but cloud integration is not mentioned.
"
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adheres to the **ISO 23247 – Digital Twin Framework for Manufacturing**, a four-part standard published in 2021. Part 2 of the standard defines a **reference architecture (RA)** based on an **entity-based model**, where functionality is distributed across four primary entities: **Observable Manufacturing Elements (OMEs)**, **Data Collection and Device Control Entities**, **Digital Twin Entities (DTEs)**, and **User Entities**. Each entity is further decomposed into **Functional Entities (FEs)**, enabling modular implementation. The standard does not mandate a specific interoperability protocol but provides **OPC Unified Architecture (UA)** as an example implementation choice for interfacing FEs with OMEs or user interfaces. While ISO 23247 establishes a **compliance framework** for Digital Twin development in manufacturing, it does not detail **certification requirements** or **conformity assessment procedures**. The standard focuses on **structural and functional interoperability** rather than regulatory compliance or industry-specific certifications."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The provided documents reference **safety-critical considerations** for an **Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) system** within the context of **ISO 15622:2018** [35], which outlines performance requirements and test procedures for intelligent transport systems. While no explicit mention of **ISO 26262** (functional safety for road vehicles) or **FMEA (Failure Mode and Effects Analysis)** is made, the system's **safety-critical nature** is implied through its reliance on **real-time control mechanisms** and **latency-sensitive operations** (as referenced in **3GPP TS 22.185 [36]**, which specifies **V2X (Vehicle-to-Everything) service requirements** with strict **latency constraints**—e.g., **<100ms** for safety-critical communications).

The **model-driven architecture for interactive digital twin cockpits** [37] emphasizes **safety assurance** through **interactive validation**, but does not detail specific **cybersecurity protocols** (e.g., encryption, access control) or **threat mitigation strategies**. The documents do not provide evidence of **authentication systems**, **fail-safe mechanisms**, or **risk assessment methodologies** beyond the implicit need for **deterministic performance** in safety-critical applications. **No explicit compliance frameworks** (e.g., ISO 26262, IEC 62443) or **data protection regulations** (e.g., GDPR) are mentioned in the provided context."
]
